name: Greg's Favourite Pasta
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the dry ingredients on a floured surface, forming a well in the center.
2. Add olive oil and eggs to the well. Mix with a fork, gradually blending the dry ingredients into the wet.
3. When the mixture is too stiff to mix by hand, knead gently by hand until it reaches a very stiff consistency.
4. If necessary, add warm water in small increments.
5. Allow the dough to rest, covered, for at least 10 minutes.
6. Using a floured surface, cut pieces of dough about the size of an egg.
7. Flatten each piece with the palm of your hand.
8. Process through the pasta machine starting on the thickest setting.
9. Roll through the machine again at progressively thinner settings to form long strips the width of your machine and the proper thickness for the desired style of pasta.
10. Gently rub a small amount of flour on the surface, allow to air dry for about 30 minutes (this makes cutting easier).
11. Attach the appropriate cutting head for the desired pasta type.
12. Cut, lay out on a floured board, sprinkle lightly with flour, and allow to dry further before cooking.
13. Fresh pasta cooks much faster than dried pasta; do not overcook. If cut with a medium width head, these make great chicken and noodles or thin cuts for spaghetti.
14. For those more health-conscious, reduce the number of eggs and replace with water. A pinch of saffron can be added to return the rich golden color.
